records.ety: [OE. techen, imp. taughte, tahte, AS. tǣcean, imp. tǣhte, to show, teach, akin to tācn token. See Token.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Taught (tt); p. pr. & vb. n. Teaching.]

defs: To impart the knowledge of; to give intelligence concerning; to impart, as knowledge before unknown, or rules for practice; to inculcate as true or important; to exhibit impressively; as, to teach arithmetic, dancing, music, or the like; to teach morals.
quotes: If some men teach wicked things, it must be that others should practice them.

defs: To direct, as an instructor; to manage, as a preceptor; to guide the studies of; to instruct; to inform; to conduct through a course of studies; as, to teach a child or a class.
quotes: The village master taught his little school.

defs: To accustom; to guide; to show; to admonish.
quotes: I shall myself to herbs teach you.
They have taught their tongue to speak lies.
notes: ☞ This verb is often used with two objects, one of the person, the other of the thing; as, he taught me Latin grammar. In the passive construction, either of these objects may be retained in the objective case, while the other becomes the subject; as, I was taught Latin grammar by him; Latin grammar was taught me by him.
synonyms: admonish. See the Note under Learn

defs: To give instruction; to follow the business, or to perform the duties, of a preceptor.
quotes: And gladly would he learn, and gladly teach.
The priests thereof teach for hire.
